CONTENT.Club career: Product of Lazio's youth system, he made his Serie A debut for the senior team on 8 January 2017, as a substitute replacing Lucas Biglia in the 82nd minute of a 1–0 home win against Crotone at the Stadio Olimpico.On 18 August 2017, Rossi was signed by Serie B club Salernitana on a season-long loan deal. On 26 August he made his Serie B debut for Salernitana as a substitute replacing Riccardo Bocalon in the 88th minute of a 0–0 away draw against Venezia. On 9 September, Rossi played his first match as a starter in a 1–0 away defeat against Carpi, he was replaced by Alejandro Rodriguez in the 46th minute. On 24 October he played his first entire match for Salernitana, a 3–2 away win over Novara. On 4 November, Rossi scored his first professional goal in the 14th minute and he scored again in the 48th minute of a 2–2 home draw against Bari. Rossi ended his loan to Salernitana with 27 appearances, 2 goals and 3 assists.On 16 January 2019, Rossi joined on loan to Serie B club Venezia until 30 June 2019. Five days later, on 21 January he made his debut for Venezia in a 1–1 away draw against Spezia, he was replaced by Cristiano Lombardi in the 51st minute.On 19 July 2019, Rossi joined Serie B club Juve Stabia on loan until 30 June 2020.On 19 August 2020, Rossi moved on loan to Serie C club Viterbese.On 4 January 2022, he joined Monopoli in Serie C on loan until the end of the season. Monopoli held an option to purchase his rights at the end of the loan (with a counter-option for Lazio to retain the rights) and an option to extend the loan further.On 14 August 2022, Rossi was loaned by Monterosi.
CONTENT.International career: Rossi was a youth international for italy.

SUMMARY: Alessandro Rossi (born 3 January 1997) is an Italian footballer who plays as a striker for Serie D club Monterosi.
